description_long: This object is a towed artillery piece, likely an anti-tank or field gun, featuring a long barrel mounted on a robust carriage. The carriage is supported by two large spoked wheels and two long stabilizing trails that extend rearward, which can be spread for firing or closed for transport. The central section includes a gun shield for limited crew protection and a visible breech mechanism at the rear of the barrel. The overall construction is predominantly metal, with a dark, matte finish and utilitarian design. The asset is placed on a circular patch of textured ground, simulating a prepared firing position.
description: A towed artillery gun with a long barrel, large wheels, and extended stabilizing trails, finished in dark matte metal.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gun barrel, shield, and both stabilizing trails spread wide.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting one wheel, the length of the barrel, and the extended trails.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the breech, shield, and the symmetrical spread of the trails.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, showing the other wheel and the full length of the trails.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One main cannon mount at the center front of the carriage.
attribute_armor_plating: A single gun shield at the front, providing partial frontal protection; no other visible armor plating.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; surface is a solid dark color.
attribute_tire_type: Two large spoked wheels with solid rubber tires, no visible tread pattern.
